title: sshd won't start at all: 'no hostkeys available'
description_md:
Other users trying to SSH into this box get refused at the TCP level -- sshd isn't even listening. Starting it manually shows `sshd: no hostkeys available -- exiting`. A broad "permissions audit" script ran across `/etc` recently. You're already on the box as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`); find what's actually stopping sshd from using its own host keys, and get it running.

You're done when sshd is actually running and listening on port 22.
